description: Open 2D/2H/2S with 6+ cards and 5-11 HCP, responses include Ogust
purpose: Preempt opponents with a descriptive opening while giving partner enough information to judge game prospects via Ogust

authority.label: ACBL SAYC — Weak Twos are standard SAYC, Ogust responses are the common agreement

teaching.tradeoff: Using 2D/2H/2S as weak openings means you can't use them for strong two-bids.
teaching.principle: Preemption works by consuming bidding space — make opponents guess at a high level with incomplete information.

commonMistakes.text: Do not open a Weak Two with a five-card suit.
commonMistakes.reason: The convention presumes a real six-card suit, so stretching on five cards steals space without giving partner a trustworthy picture.

commonMistakes.text: Do not open a Weak Two with an outside four-card major.
commonMistakes.reason: A side four-card major often points toward a better one-level opening or a hand that is too playable to preempt immediately.

commonMistakes.text: Do not open a Weak Two with a void or a strong side suit.
commonMistakes.reason: Extreme shape or extra playing strength usually means the hand belongs in a different descriptive route than a routine preempt.

commonMistakes.text: Do not forget the Ogust response map.
commonMistakes.reason: If your partnership plays Ogust, 2NT asks opener to show minimum/maximum plus suit quality, so the step responses have to be agreed in advance.

definitions.description: Count of A, K, Q in hearts (raw top-3 count used by Ogust response partitioning)
Count of A, K, Q in spades (raw top-3 count used by Ogust response partitioning)
Count of A, K, Q in diamonds (raw top-3 count used by Ogust response partitioning)
Hearts suit quality passes authority: 2 of top 3 honors (A/K/Q) OR 3 of top 5 honors (A/K/Q/J/T)
Spades suit quality passes authority: 2 of top 3 honors (A/K/Q) OR 3 of top 5 honors (A/K/Q/J/T)
Diamonds suit quality passes authority: 2 of top 3 honors (A/K/Q) OR 3 of top 5 honors (A/K/Q/J/T)
Opener is minimum for weak two (5-7 NV, 6-7 vul)
Opener is maximum for weak two (8-11 HCP)
HCP in weak two opening range (5-11 NV, 6-11 vul)
All three top honors (AKQ) in hearts
All three top honors (AKQ) in spades
All three top honors (AKQ) in diamonds

Has 5+ cards in a suit other than spades
Has 5+ cards in a suit other than diamonds

Opener has 3+ support for a non-diamonds suit

summaryCard.trigger: You open a weak two, partner responds
summaryCard.partnership: This module implements a single HCP range across vulnerabilities. The canonical treatment is tighter vulnerable, roughly six to ten, versus looser non-vulnerable, roughly five to eleven; that vulnerability-sensitive split is deferred until the fact catalog can express it.

whenToUse.gloss: Use the diamond opening with a long suit and preemptive strength when you want to narrow the auction at once.
Use the heart opening with a long suit when taking away room matters more than keeping every natural call available.
Use the spade opening with a long suit when the hand wants to preempt first and explain later.

workedAuctions.label: Ogust sorts the hand for game

calls.rationale: Opener shows a weak two in hearts with a long suit inside the partnership range.
Responder uses Ogust to ask about hand quality before choosing partscore or game.
The reply shows a maximum with a good suit, pushing the deal toward game.
Responder accepts the maximum description and places the contract in game.
This opening is wrong when opener really has full opening strength and should start with a stronger call.
Responder treats the bid as a normal preemptive opening and raises to the expected game level.
The auction is already compressed, so opener has no clean way to recover the extra power that was hidden at the start.

items.ourAction: Keep the weak two meaning and compete with whatever redouble and runout style the partnership has agreed.
items.note: Most pairs still treat simple raises and feature asks as fit-showing, not as penalty actions.

items.ourAction: Compete naturally and stop treating every new call as an Ogust relay.
items.note: Once the opponents consume space, descriptive raises and cue bids usually replace the clean ask structure.

items.opponentAction: No interference after partner's weak two opening
items.ourAction: Responder's two notrump is Ogust or a feature ask by partnership agreement, while a new suit is forcing for one round.
items.note: Agree which asking structure is on before using the artificial two-notrump continuation.

items.recommendation: With a weak hand, pass or make the cheapest obstructive raise with support.
items.note: The old suit rows collapsed into one shared weak-two baseline because vulnerability and opened-suit partitions are not catalog-backed yet.

items.recommendation: With invitational values, raise constructively or ask with two notrump when hand quality matters.
items.note: Ogust and invitational raises now share a strength bucket instead of separate suit rows.

items.recommendation: With game values, jump to game when the fit is clear or use the ask structure before committing.
items.note: The shared bucket keeps diamonds, hearts, and spades together and loses suit-specific examples.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Strong two clubs uses the same level for the opposite hand type: forcing power instead of a preempt.
Choose a natural opening when the hand is too balanced, too strong, or too short in the suit for a weak two.

teaching.purpose: Open light hands with a long suit at the 2-level to preempt opponents, and use the Ogust convention to describe hand strength and suit quality
teaching.whenToUse: Open 2D/2H/2S with 6+ cards in the suit and 5-11 HCP. As responder, use 2NT (Ogust) to ask about opener's hand, raise to game with 16+ HCP and fit, or invite with 14-15 HCP and fit.
whenNotToUse: 2C is reserved for strong openings — never open 2C as a weak two
With a hand too strong for a weak two (12+ HCP) — open at the 1-level instead
After opponent interference — standard competitive actions apply
teaching.tradeoff: 2-level openings lose their natural strong meaning in favor of preemptive action
teaching.principle: Weak twos simultaneously obstruct opponents and describe a narrow hand type (6-card suit, limited strength) that partner can evaluate precisely
teaching.roles: Opener describes; responder captains. After Ogust 2NT, opener further classifies along strength and quality dimensions
